Author bio: SF by day, NYC by blood, PDX at heart. Partner @BessemerVP

Article content: {"cover_media":"3_2066379290246270976","preview_text":"This is a comeback story. The founder-CEO left and returned. The company transformed from SaaS to AI agent, rebranded from Intercom to Fin, and went from decelerating growth to one of the","entities":{"mentions":[{"start":378,"end":385,"username":"fin_ai"},{"start":396,"end":405,"username":"intercom"},{"start":449,"end":460,"username":"salesforce"},{"start":0,"end":11,"username":"BessemerVP"},{"start":129,"end":136,"username":"eoghan"},{"start":138,"end":149,"username":"destraynor"},{"start":592,"end":604,"username":"fergal_reid"},{"start":50,"end":57,"username":"eoghan"},{"start":59,"end":70,"username":"destraynor"},{"start":161,"end":171,"username":"ethankurz"},{"start":177,"end":185,"username":"bdeeter"}],"urls":[{"text":"https://x.com/eoghan/status/2028522852044206258?s=20"}]},"plain_text":"This is a comeback story. The founder-CEO left and returned. The company transformed from SaaS to AI agent, rebranded from Intercom to Fin, and went from decelerating growth to one of the fastest-growing AI companies ever, accelerating for more than 10 consecutive quarters while operating at scale. All driven by the team's brave decisions to go all-in on agentic enterprise.

@fin_ai (formerly @intercom) has signed an agreement to be acquired by @salesforce for ~$3.6 billion.
@BessemerVP first invested in Intercom in 2014. It was one of the most exciting young SaaS companies in the world. It was led by @eoghan, @destraynor and a team with an unusually clear vision of what software should feel like.  It grew into a category defining business for customer communication.

Then, like many SaaS companies after the ZIRP era, the company was humbled. Growth stalled. Eoghan returned as CEO in October 2022 after a two year hiatus. One month later, ChatGPT launched and he recognized overnight that everything had changed.
What followed was one of the more radical corporate transformations. 

The team didn't just add AI features to Intercom. They dismantled Intercom to make way for Fin, the AI customer agent. The company rewrote its values, refocused its mission, restructured the team and resources to go all in on Fin — all while it was still little more than an idea. They were first to do outcome-based pricing in the category and migrated tens of thousands of customers to it, even at the expense of a large business line. They launched a new brand. They built a world class team of AI researchers (led by @fergal_reid). They launched Apex. 
They accelerated growth rate quarter after quarter, transforming into one of the world’s fastest growing AI companies. 

A decade after its original investment, Bessemer doubled down on the company and team.
Because this is what “founder mode” is all about. @eoghan, @destraynor, Ciaran, and the entire team have written the clearest playbook we've seen for what it takes to win in the AI transition. Relentless conviction. The willingness to destroy revenue to build something better. And the product and cultural discipline to pull it off at scale. 
Congrats, team! Thank you for building an important company and for creating a blueprint for what is possible. 

The agentic enterprise is here.

PS - thanks to @ethankurz  and @bdeeter for leading our initial investment in 2014 and serving the company. That positioned us to partner more deeply many years later. ","title":"Fin: The Result of Founder Mode"}
